title: GuyNode API v2 Release Notes: Streaming Layers and Vector Tiles
excerpt: Technical overview of the new API features, including improved latency for vector tile delivery and new authentication scopes.
content:
We are excited to announce the release of GuyNode API v2. This update focuses on performance and interoperability for developers building external GIS applications.

Major Changes

  • Vector Tiles Support: Endpoints now support MVT (Mapbox Vector Tiles) for administrative boundaries, reducing payload size by 70%.
  • Streaming GeoJSON: Large datasets can now be streamed, improving initial load time for web maps.
  • Scoped Keys: API keys can now be restricted to specific referrers or IP ranges.

Check the Developer Documentation for the full migration guide.

categories.name: Engineering
categories.description: Technical updates and API release notes.

title: Local Geo Units in Guyana Need Business Workflows
excerpt: Neighborhood Democratic Councils (NDCs) struggle with revenue collection. Integrating simple GIS workflows can revolutionize how local properties are managed and taxed.
content:
Neighborhood Democratic Councils (NDCs) in Guyana form the backbone of local governance, yet many operate with outdated, paper-based workflows. One of the most critical challenges they face is revenue collection—specifically, the ability to accurately track property rates and taxes.

The Spatial Gap in Revenue Collection

Without a spatial view of their jurisdiction, NDCs rely on lists that may not reflect new construction, subdivisions, or changes in land use. A GIS-based approach allows councils to:

  • Visualize tax compliance on a map.
  • Identify unregistered properties.
  • Plan collection routes efficiently.

At GuyNode, we are piloting a "Business Workflow" layer for NDCs that integrates property boundaries with simplified status codes (Paid, Arrears, Unregistered).

title: Web Geovisualizing the Role of Villages within Guyana’s Local Government System
excerpt: Using web maps to tell the historical and administrative story of Guyana’s village movement and their modern-day boundaries.
content:
The village movement in Guyana is not just historical; it is structural. Villages remain a primary unit of social and administrative organization. However, the exact boundaries of these historic units are often contested or unclear in digital formats.

Geovisualization offers a bridge between the historical record and modern administration. By digitizing survey maps from the 19th and 20th centuries, we can reconstruct the spatial logic of the village movement.

This project aims to overlay historic village grants with modern NDC boundaries, highlighting discrepancies and continuity in local governance.

title: Thematic Mapping: Georgetown, Guyana as a Top-Class Caribbean City
excerpt: Visualizing urban density, zoning, and infrastructure to understand Georgetown’s potential and challenges as a regional capital.
content:
Georgetown is transforming. With the oil and gas boom, the city is experiencing rapid verticalization and sprawl. Thematic mapping provides the lens through which we can understand this change.

Key Indicators Mapped

  • Commercial Density: The shift of business districts southwards.
  • Traffic Flow: Choke points in the classic grid layout.
  • Green Spaces: The preservation of canals and gardens.

By comparing Georgetown’s spatial metrics with other Caribbean capitals like Kingston and Port of Spain, we can better advocate for zoning policies that preserve its character while enabling growth.

title: The State of Open Data in the Caribbean: A 2023 Retrospective
excerpt: A comparative analysis of Spatial Data Infrastructure (SDI) implementations across CARICOM nations.
content:
Open data is a policy choice. In 2023, we conducted a review of national SDIs across the Caribbean Community (CARICOM). The results show a fragmented landscape but one full of potential.

While Jamaica and Trinidad & Tobago have established mature portals, nations like Guyana and Suriname are rapidly catching up through grassroots initiatives like GuyNode.

This report analyzes accessibility, licensing, and update frequency across 12 member states.

categories.name: Open Data
categories.description: Research and advocacy for open spatial data.
